name: Do I need a permit to replace or install an exterior door in Pacifica, Daly City, or nearby cities?
headline: Do I need a permit to replace or install an exterior door in Pacifica, Daly City, or nearby cities?

mainEntity.name: Do I need a permit to replace or install an exterior door in Pacifica, Daly City, or nearby cities?
mainEntity.text: Do I need a permit to replace or install an exterior door in Pacifica, Daly City, or nearby cities?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most of the time, yes, you do need a permit to install a new exterior door, especially if you are changing the size of the opening, moving the location, or altering structural framing. Cities on the Peninsula and coastside, like Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and Half Moon Bay, treat door changes that affect the structure or energy performance as building work, not just cosmetic. If you’re simply swapping an old door for a new one of the same size and type, and not touching the framing, many homeowners do this without a permit. But if you need to cut into stucco, move studs, widen or shrink the opening, add sidelights, or change from window to door, that usually triggers a permit and inspection. Your city’s building department can confirm with one quick phone call. Permits matter because they ensure the door is properly flashed against water, safely anchored, and meets energy and egress rules. Skipping permits can cause problems when you sell your home or file an insurance claim after damage. Pitfalls to avoid: assuming interior and exterior doors are treated the same (exterior is much stricter), trusting online advice from other states, or starting demo before you know what’s allowed. Always check your local rules first, especially in coastal areas with strong wind and rain. Even if you hire a contractor, ask who is pulling the permit and make sure inspections are scheduled and passed.
